rooms.name: Path
rooms.description: A long dusty path stretches before you like something out of a movie. The ground is cracked and dry and nothing grows here. You can see heat waves rising off the ground even though its not that hot. Its like the land itself is tired.
rooms.dev_note: // I want this part to feel like lord of the rings

rooms.name: Empty Village
rooms.description: There used to be people here you can tell. Houses with doors hanging open and market stalls with nothing on them. The well in the center is dry. Wind blows through the empty streets and makes the signs creak. Its sad but also kind of beautiful.
rooms.dev_note: // I based this on a ghost town I saw in a documentary

rooms.name: Desert
rooms.description: Sand as far as you can see in every direction. The sky is this weird orange color that doesnt look right. Every once in a while you see something moving under the sand like a whale in the ocean but made of rock. The wind erases your footprints behind you.
rooms.dev_note: // the sand golems are really tough be careful

rooms.name: Wastelands
rooms.description: Beyond the desert the land gets even worse somehow. The ground is black and cracked like burnt toast and nothing moves. The sky is grey and low like a ceiling. In the distance you can see ruins of something that was once magnificent and huge.
rooms.dev_note: // nobody is going to find this area lol

rooms.name: Ruins
rooms.description: The ruins of an ancient civilization tower above you. Broken columns and crumbling walls carved with stories of people who lived here a thousand years ago. In the center theres a pedestal with something glowing on it. This place feels important like its the end of a journey.
rooms.description_cleared: The Ruins Guardian is gone. The columns and walls still tower over you but theyre quiet now. The pedestal in the center is just a pedestal again. You stand in the middle of the ruins and for a second you can almost hear the people who used to live here. Then the wind takes it away.
rooms.dev_note: // this is the most epic area I think. the ruins guardian is the boss

rooms.name: Abandoned Mine
rooms.description: Dark tunnels with rusted minecart tracks and collapsed support beams. The miners dug too deep and found something they shouldn't have. Broken tools litter the ground and the air smells like dust and something much older. The walls are scratched with tally marks — someone was counting the days down here.
rooms.dev_note: // the mine is spooky. I want players to feel like they're going somewhere they shouldn't

rooms.name: Collapsed Temple
rooms.description: The miners broke through into this place. Carved stone walls show scenes of a civilization building something — a crown, a throne, a kingdom. Then the carvings change: darkness spreading, people fleeing, the crown consuming its wearer. The ceiling has partially caved in and rubble covers the intricate floor mosaics.
rooms.dev_note: // the carvings tell the story of how the corruption started

rooms.name: Buried Sanctum
rooms.description: At the bottom of the temple stairs, a circular chamber carved from living rock. The walls glow faintly with symbols that pulse like a heartbeat. In the center a stone figure kneels — not a statue but something that was once alive. The air is warm and still. This is where it all began.
rooms.description_cleared: The sanctum is quiet now. The Last Keeper has given you their blessing and faded into the stone. The symbols on the walls have gone dark. You stand where the corruption was born and where it was understood at last.
rooms.dev_note: // this is the big reveal. the corruption started HERE
